macro_context.market_sentiment: Risk-on with mixed undercurrents: S&P 500 +0.46% to 7,168, Dow +1.49% to 49,588, Nasdaq +0.21% on April 30. Big tech earnings were bifurcated (Alphabet +9%, Meta -9%, Microsoft -5%), while industrials (Caterpillar) led the Dow. April is on track for best monthly gain since late 2020.
macro_context.fed_outlook: FOMC held rates at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29 with an unusual 8-4 split: Miran dissented for a 25bp cut while Hammack, Kashkari, and Logan opposed including an easing bias. Elevated inflation tied to Middle East energy disruptions and Powell's transition to a 'low profile' governor role as Warsh takes over create near-term policy uncertainty.
macro_context.geopolitical: US-Iran conflict closed the Strait of Hormuz (March 4), pushing Brent crude above $100/bbl — the largest oil market disruption in history per the IEA. Peace talks stalled in late April. IMF downgraded 2026 global growth to 3.1% and raised headline inflation forecast to 4.4%. Goldman Sachs itself raised its Brent forecast to $90/bbl by year-end.
key_events: Fed leadership transition: Powell steps aside; Warsh confirmation hearings expected Q2-Q3 2026
Q2 2026 mega-cap tech and bank earnings season begins July 2026
US-Iran ceasefire/peace negotiations remain binary risk for oil and broader risk sentiment
Brent crude trading ~$90+ — sustained high oil could reignite inflation and delay Fed cuts
IMF April 2026 WEO: global growth 3.1%, inflation 4.4% — stagflation risk elevated

ticker_news.headline: Goldman Sachs Q1 2026 EPS $17.55 beats estimate $16.47; revenue $17.23B (+14% YoY); annualized ROTE 19.8%
ticker_news.source: Goldman Sachs Investor Relations / CNBC, April 13 2026

ticker_news.headline: Goldman Sachs fixed income revenue fell 10% in Q1 2026, missing analyst estimates by $910M as JPMorgan (+21%) and Morgan Stanley (+29%) thrived
Goldman Sachs returns to ETF market-making but only for top-performing funds — selective re-entry into competitive business
Goldman Sachs forecasts 100 IPOs raising $160B in 2026, revised down from 120 deals previously amid market volatility
Goldman Sachs appointed Akila Raman as global head of private and alternatives capital markets — insider continuity signal
ticker_news.source: Simply Wall St / Goldman Sachs, April 2026

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: No upgrades or downgrades in April 2026. Most recent action was JP Morgan changing price target from $625 to $750 in October 2025. Current breakdown out of 37 analysts: 8 Buy, 17 Hold, 1 Sell. Price target range $600-$1,125; median $982.50. Sentiment cautious given FICC miss and macro uncertainty, partially offset by strong overall Q1 beat.

web_context.competitor_activity: JPMorgan Chase posted FICC revenue of $7.1B (+21% YoY) in Q1 2026, and Morgan Stanley fixed income jumped 29% — both sharply outpacing Goldman's 10% decline in that business, raising market-share questions in Goldman's historically dominant segment. Overall Finance sector Q1 earnings growth expected at +19.9% YoY on +9.2% revenues, meaning Goldman's headline beat is partly offset by relative FICC underperformance versus peers.
web_context.earnings_info: Q1 2026 already reported (April 13 2026): EPS $17.55 (beat estimate $16.47), revenue $17.23B (+14% YoY), ROTE 19.8%. Next earnings: Q2 2026, expected approximately July 14-22, 2026 (Before Market Open). Q2 2026 consensus revenue estimate: $15.68B. No Q2 EPS estimate surfaced in search results.
